headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Vanuatu (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 35.9 / Developing has UPWARD DIMENSIONAL PRESSURE from strong in-2026 INT and BND signals — Vanuatu's ICJ Climate Initiative is a category-defining international-norms-leadership action that strengthens BND and INT beyond the published Developing-band profile. (1) Westminster parliamentary democracy; small Melanesian Pacific state (~315,000 population); (2) ICJ Climate Initiative: led 132-nation coalition for UNGA Resolution; July 2025 ICJ landmark Advisory Opinion on state climate obligations unanimous; 2026 follow-up resolution circulated by Vanuatu (May 20 UN consideration); US 'strongly objects' but Vanuatu refused to withdraw — values-consistency under direct great-power pressure (Al Jazeera Feb 2026); (3) Climate-existential threat as Pacific archipelago; sea-level rise; tropical cyclone exposure (Cyclones Pam 2015, Harold 2020, Judy/Kevin 2023); (4) BTI 2024 designates as 'partly democratic'; political instability (frequent no-confidence motions) is structural; (5) Freedom House: Free designation; (6) Limited capacity small-state development challenges; no documented atrocity-level conduct. The ICJ Climate Initiative is the strongest INT-dimension signal documented for any Pacific micro-state in the active rotation. Internal scoring shows BND 3.0 and INT 3.0 are defensible (vs. published 2.5 each). Reconstructed internal composite 41.0 (+5.1 from published). Marginally material delta. Per the day-3 sustained-conduct dimensional-credit rule articulated in the Ukraine 2026-05-11 proposal: the ICJ Initiative is a sustained 2023-2026 multi-year climate-leadership pattern that earns dimensional upward credit; the resulting composite delta is at the materiality threshold. Boundary case: internal 41.0 crosses the Functional floor by 1.0pt. DECISION: confirm at published 35.9 with explicit upward dimensional pressure documented; recommend Functional band crossing watch for next rotation cycle (60-90 days). The ICJ May 20 follow-up resolution outcome is the next material scoring trigger.
dimensional_upward_pressure_note: Internal scoring shows BND 3.0 and INT 3.0 are defensible based on the multi-year ICJ Climate Initiative: 2023 UNGA Resolution leadership (132-nation coalition), 2025 unanimous ICJ Advisory Opinion, 2026 follow-up resolution with US pressure withstood. The ICJ Initiative represents (1) sustained international-norms-leadership action across 3+ years — anchor-3 BND scope-clarity behavior; (2) values-consistency under direct US pressure refused — anchor-3 INT consistency-under-pressure behavior. Internal composite at +0.5 each = 21 sum / 8 mean = 2.625 → ((2.625-1)/4)*100 = 40.625 → 40.6. Reconstructed internal composite 40.6 is 0.6pt above the Functional floor (40.0) — boundary case. Delta from published 35.9 is +4.7 (just below the 5.0pt materiality threshold). Per conservative first-baseline scoring rule, the upward pressure is documented but the published 35.9 is confirmed; Functional band crossing watch is flagged for next rotation cycle. The ICJ May 20 follow-up resolution outcome could push the dimensional pressure into material upgrade territory.
key_evidence: ICJ Climate Initiative 2023-2026: Vanuatu led 132-nation coalition for UNGA Resolution requesting ICJ Advisory Opinion on state climate obligations. Multi-year sustained international-norms-leadership action.

July 2025: ICJ delivered unanimous landmark Advisory Opinion on states' obligations to address climate change. Ruling: states legally required to protect the climate system, prevent transboundary harm, regulate emissions-driving activities.

February 2026 (in-2026 carry-over): Vanuatu introduced draft UN resolution operationalizing the ICJ Advisory Opinion; US State Department cable said Trump administration 'strongly objects' — Vanuatu refused to withdraw. Values-consistency under direct great-power pressure (Al Jazeera Feb 14 2026).

March 2026 (carry-over): HRW issued 'Governments Should Support Vanuatu's UN Climate Resolution' urging international support. UN experts (OHCHR press release March 2026) urged states to support General Assembly resolution operationalising ICJ Advisory Opinion.
April 2026 (carry-over): Amnesty International analysis: 'A UN climate change resolution that may shift climate accountability for decades.'
May 7 2026 (just outside window): ClimateChangeNews 'ICJ follow-up resolution is a test of climate leadership at the UN.' Forward signal.
May 20 2026 (forward signal, post-window): UN member states to consider operationalising resolution. Next material scoring trigger.
Climate-existential threat: Pacific archipelago; sea-level rise; tropical cyclone exposure (Pam 2015, Harold 2020, Judy/Kevin 2023). Structural EMP context.

Westminster parliamentary democracy; Free designation (Freedom House); frequent no-confidence motions (political instability is structural but rights-respecting). No documented atrocity-level conduct.
No in-window May 10-11 events but the ICJ Initiative is an active multi-month structural-scoring input.

evidence.finding: Vanuatu's leadership documented: 132-nation coalition for 2023 UNGA Resolution; 2025 ICJ Advisory Opinion; 2026 follow-up resolution.

evidence.source: HRW — Governments Should Support Vanuatu's UN Climate Resolution
evidence.finding: March 2026 HRW endorsement of Vanuatu's leadership; international-norms-leadership recognized.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — US pressures Vanuatu at UN over ICJ's landmark climate change ruling
evidence.finding: February 2026: US 'strongly objects' to Vanuatu's resolution; Vanuatu refused to withdraw. Values-consistency under direct great-power pressure.

evidence.source: Amnesty International — How Vanuatu's proposed UN climate change resolution may shift climate accountability
evidence.finding: April 2026 Amnesty analysis: resolution represents potential decades-long shift in climate accountability framework.

evidence.source: OHCHR — UN experts urge states to support General Assembly resolution operationalising ICJ Advisory Opinion
evidence.finding: March 2026 UN experts' explicit endorsement of operationalising ICJ Advisory Opinion via UNGA resolution.

evidence.source: ClimateChangeNews — ICJ follow-up resolution is a test of climate leadership at the UN
evidence.finding: May 7 2026 (just outside window): May 20 UN vote framed as climate-leadership test.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 sustained. A1 detection: small-state institutional capacity limited; A4 amplification: Vanuatu's diplomatic amplification of climate-vulnerability narrative is anchor-3 international-amplification but limited domestic-detection infrastructure caps overall at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 sustained. E1 affective resonance: climate-vulnerability narrative is empathy-grounded; E4 validation: lived experience of cyclone harm acknowledged.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 sustained. AC1 responsiveness: cyclone response capacity limited but functional; AC4 resource mobilization: small-state limitations.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 sustained. EQ1 universality: Westminster framework with multi-party politics; EQ2 distributional awareness: gender-equality gaps in Melanesian-cultural context partially addressed.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 published score CONFIRMED. INTERNAL ASSESSMENT 3.0 (+0.5 dimensional credit for sustained 2023-2026 ICJ Climate Initiative international-norms-leadership; B3 scope clarity: Vanuatu maintained the climate-accountability scope under US opposition; B4 refusal ethics: refused to withdraw resolution under US pressure). Internal 3.0 anchors the dimensional upward pressure; published 2.5 retained at composite level due to sub-threshold composite delta (+4.7).
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 sustained. AB1 harm acknowledgment: state acknowledges climate harm at international level; AB2 transparency: partial.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 sustained. S2 long-term impact: ICJ Initiative is a multi-year structural-policy initiative; but Vanuatu's domestic institutional capacity caps overall SYS at 2.5.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 published score CONFIRMED. INTERNAL ASSESSMENT 3.0 (+0.5 dimensional credit for sustained values-consistency under direct US great-power pressure: Vanuatu refused to withdraw resolution despite US 'strong objection'; I1 consistency-under-pressure anchor-3; I2 non-performance: declared climate-leadership commitment is operationally enacted, not just rhetorical). Internal 3.0 anchors the dimensional upward pressure; published 2.5 retained at composite level.

math_check.materiality_threshold_check: Delta +3.1 (internal 39.1 vs published 35.9) is SUB-THRESHOLD (below 5.0pt materiality floor). Confirm at 35.9. Internal 39.1 is 0.9pt below the Functional floor (40.0) — boundary case watch.
math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly at both levels. Internal composite 39.1 is 0.9pt below the Functional floor — boundary watch flagged. Developing band sustained.

boundary_flag: BOUNDARY CASE WATCH. Internal composite 39.1 is 0.9pt below the Functional floor (40.0). Within the 1.0pt boundary-flag window. The May 20 UN vote on the ICJ follow-up resolution is the next material scoring trigger that could push the dimensional upward pressure into Functional band crossing.
negativeSignals: Climate-existential vulnerability (sea-level rise, tropical cyclones); structural-poverty challenges; political instability (frequent no-confidence motions); small-state institutional capacity limits; limited domestic detection/accountability infrastructure; gender-equality gaps in Melanesian cultural context.
positiveSignals: ICJ Climate Initiative 2023-2026 multi-year sustained international-norms-leadership; 132-nation coalition built; unanimous 2025 ICJ Advisory Opinion delivered; 2026 follow-up resolution sustained under direct US pressure; HRW, Amnesty, UN experts public endorsement; Free designation (Freedom House); Westminster democratic framework; no documented atrocity-level conduct.
sector_context: Pacific Melanesia cluster. Vanuatu is the leading climate-diplomacy entity in the Pacific micro-state cluster. The ICJ Initiative is the strongest INT-dimension signal documented for any Pacific micro-state in the active rotation. The boundary-case status (0.9pt below Functional floor) raises a calibration question consistent with the May 10 Pacific boundary cluster observation (Timor-Leste, Kiribati, Marshall Islands at 39.1). Vanuatu's profile may indicate that the Developing-band Pacific micro-state baseline is conservatively calibrated relative to international-norms-leadership behavior.

methodology_consistency_check: First-baseline assessment applies the day-3 sustained-conduct dimensional-credit-under-sub-threshold-composite rule articulated in the Ukraine 2026-05-11 proposal: dimensional credit is awarded for documented sustained positive conduct (ICJ Initiative); the resulting composite delta is sub-threshold for change-proposal generation; the published score is confirmed with the dimensional upward pressure documented. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
